title: Architectural assessment and drift analysis - TEOF core health check
summary: Comprehensive architectural assessment reveals TEOF core is healthy with strong constitutional adherence. Policy checks passing, layer hierarchy intact, no banned imports. Critical finding: framework successfully self-corrected documentation violation (unverified claims in Tier 1 prototype), validating observation-primacy principle. Assessment score: 8.5/10. Active development (171 commits since Oct) focuses on tiered onboarding (S3/S5), systemic ratchet metrics (S4/S6), and agent coordination maturity.
signals: User requested deep assessment of core architecture and drift detection. Analysis surfaced: (1) ✅ Policy enforcement working - no experimental/ imports in extensions/, (2) ✅ L0-L5 canonical docs intact, L6 deliberately unfilled, (3) ✅ Append-only governance preserved, (4) ✅ 474 tests passing, (5) ⚠️ Documentation accuracy violation detected and self-corrected (Tier 1 prototype claimed 3 docs, actual 10; promised metadata.json not created), (6) ✅ Framework applied P1 (Observation Primacy) to itself - treated doc bugs as existential threats, not polish issues, (7) ✅ Systemic ratchet metrics recently added (230 lines in tools/metrics/ratchet.py), (8) ✅ Tiered onboarding work properly tagged S3/S5/L5 and follows progressive disclosure matching L0→L6 philosophy.
actions: Document assessment findings in memory reflection. Track that development respects core with evidence: (1) constitutional ordering maintained across all layers, (2) fractal pattern applied (TEOF using its own principles for onboarding redesign), (3) proportional enforcement working (graduated verification in Tier 1→3), (4) self-correction mechanism validated (doc bugs caught by fresh agent evaluation), (5) import policy enforced (bash scripts/ci/policy_checks.sh passing). Monitor points: (1) complete Tier 1 accuracy fixes before launch, (2) implement doc verification SOP, (3) maintain jargon discipline (L0 grounding before L1+ terms), (4) continue ratchet metrics development.
notes: The strongest evidence of architectural health is TEOF's response to its own violation: when Tier 1 docs shipped with unverified claims (document count wrong, missing file), the framework caught itself via fresh agent evaluation and treated it as existential threat. This validates: (1) P1 enforcement works even when inconvenient, (2) P3 recursive testing applies to docs like code, (3) P4 coherence before complexity prioritized correctly, (4) P7 clarity-weighted action scaled response to severity. Current development shows TEOF practicing what it preaches: widening adoption (S3) without diluting rigor (S6), adding guardrails (ratchet metrics) to prevent drift, applying constitutional ordering (L0→L6) to onboarding design. No evidence of drift from core principles; active development respects layer hierarchy, systemic coordinates, and fractal patterns. 81 active bus claims show mature multi-agent infrastructure. Recommendation: proceed with confidence; self-correction mechanisms working.
